_comment: Coach arc. Same schema and mechanic as the others, different skin and story: a night train. The conductor sent you forward to coach 8, and you walk carriage after identical carriage. Turn back if a coach has changed; keep moving forward if it hasn't. Motion is part of the unease.

meta.title: Coach 8
meta.tagline: The conductor said coach 8. The train never seems to reach it.
meta.attempt: {n} times through this train, and this time it reached your stop.
meta.attempt_first: First ride, and the coach never doubled back.

dim: The cabin lights are dimmed for night, a soft amber wash.
Low night-lighting glows along the luggage racks, as always.
The overhead lights are down to their amber night setting.
The coach is lit the same soft gold as the last.

flicker: The cabin lights flutter with the motion of the train, then hold.
One strip of lighting stutters overhead.
The amber light trips off, then on again.
bright: The lights are up full, harsh and white, not the night setting.
This coach is lit far brighter than the last.
The soft amber is gone; everything glares white.
off: The cabin is dark but for the emergency floor strips.
The lights are out here; only the aisle markers glow.
Darkness, save a thin line of light along the floor.

rhythm: The rails beat their steady rhythm beneath the floor.
The train hums along, wheels ticking over the joints.
That familiar sway and clatter of a train at speed.

slowing: The rhythm of the rails is slowing. Are you stopping?
The clatter drags, as if the train is losing speed.
The beat beneath the floor stretches longer between ticks.
silence: The wheels have gone silent. You feel no motion at all.
The train sound has stopped. Dead still.
No clatter, no sway. The train might not be moving.
announce: A chime sounds, then a muffled announcement you can't make out.
The PA crackles with a voice, then cuts off.
faster: The rails beat faster than they should, a rising whine.
The train is accelerating, the clatter climbing in pitch.
Everything rattles harder; you're speeding up.

night: Beyond the window, black countryside and your own faint reflection.
The window shows only night and the ghost of the cabin.
Dark fields streak past the window, far-off lights scattered.

station: The window shows a lit platform sliding by, a station, unannounced.
Outside, a station platform passes, empty and bright.
A station slides past the glass; you never felt the train slow.
reflection: Your reflection in the glass is a half-second behind you.
The reflection in the window doesn't quite match the cabin.
Someone stands in the window's reflection who isn't in the coach.
daylight: The window is bright with daylight. It was night a moment ago.
Outside the glass it is suddenly, impossibly, day.
Sunlight fills the window where there was only dark.
tunnel: The window is pure black, a tunnel, roaring, with no end to it.
You're in a tunnel now; the glass shows only rushing dark.
Nothing but black tunnel wall streams past the window.

sleeping: A lone passenger sleeps against the far window, coat over their face.
One passenger dozes midway down the coach, head tipped back.
The same sleeping figure slumps in a window seat, as before.

gone: The seat where the passenger slept is empty now.
No one is sleeping in the coach this time.
The dozing passenger is gone; only a folded coat remains.
awake: The passenger is awake now, sitting upright, very still.
The sleeper's eyes are open, fixed on the aisle.
The passenger no longer sleeps. They watch you pass.
moved: The sleeping passenger has moved to a seat nearer the aisle.
The dozing figure is closer than before, on your side now.
The passenger sleeps in a different seat than a moment ago.
two: Two passengers sleep here now, where there was one.
There are a pair of coats-over-faces this time, not one.
A second sleeping figure has joined the first.
speaks: The sleeping passenger murmurs, without waking: 'again?'
From under the coat, a muffled voice: 'you were just here.'
The dozing figure says the name of your stop, though you told no one.

eight: The seat decals read 8A, 8B, 8C down the row.
Little brass numbers mark the seats: row 8, as always.
The coach-number panel by the door glows COACH 8.

wrong: The seat decals read row 7 now, not 8.
The brass numbers say row 6; you were sure it was 8.
missing: The seat numbers have been prised off; bare screw-holes remain.
The coach-number panel by the door is dark and blank.
There are no numbers on the seats at all this time.
letters: The seat decals read 8X, 8Y, 8Z, letters that don't belong.
The row is numbered in an alphabet you don't recognise.
The seat labels are just symbols now, not numbers.

closed: The connecting door at the coach's end is shut, its glass dark.
The powered door to the next carriage stands closed, as usual.
The end door is closed, the vestibule beyond it dim.

open: The connecting door is stuck open, cold air rushing through.
The powered door won't close; the vestibule roars beyond.
The end door gapes open onto the shaking vestibule.
locked: A red light burns on the connecting door: LOCKED.
The end door shows a locked indicator that was green before.
The door to the next coach reads DO NOT ENTER.
glass: The glass in the connecting door is fogged, a hand-print on it.
Something smears the window of the end door from the far side.
The connecting door's glass is cracked in a spider's web.

stowed: A single suitcase sits in the luggage rack by the door.
The overhead racks hold the usual scattering of bags.
One hard-shell case rides the luggage rack, as before.

gone: The luggage rack is empty now.
The suitcase by the door is gone.
There are no bags in the racks this time.
more: The luggage rack is crammed full, far more bags than before.
Cases are stacked nearly to the ceiling now.
The racks overflow with luggage that wasn't there.
open: The suitcase in the rack lies open, empty.
A case has spilled open across the luggage rack.
The hard-shell case gapes open, its contents gone.

coffee: A fold-down table by the aisle holds a half-finished coffee.
The tray table is down, a paper cup cooling on it, as always.
On the table, someone's coffee, still steaming faintly.

empty: The table is bare; the coffee cup is gone.
The tray table holds nothing now.
There's no cup on the table this time.
cold: The coffee on the table has gone cold, a skin across the top.
The cup is stone cold now, long abandoned.
The coffee sits cold and still, no steam at all.
many: The table is crowded with cups now, not one but many.
Several coffees crowd the tray table where there was one.
The table is cluttered with more cups than anyone could drink.
spilled: The coffee has spilled, a dark stain spreading over the table.
The cup is tipped, coffee running off the table's edge.
Coffee pools across the table, dripping to the floor.

steady: The train feels exactly as long as it always has.
You carry the same tired patience up the aisle, coach after coach.
Everything feels as ordinary as a night train should.

forget: For a moment you can't remember how many coaches you've walked.
You lose count of the carriages behind you, and it frightens you.
You can't recall boarding this train at all.
calm: You feel suddenly, wrongly calm, as if you'd already arrived.
A strange contentment settles over you that has no business being here.
You feel no urgency at all, and that itself feels wrong.
watched: You feel watched, though every passenger is asleep.
The certainty that someone is behind you won't leave.
Your neck prickles; you are sure you are not alone in the way you were.

repeat_inspect: You look at the {THING} again. You aren't sure why.
Your eyes go straight to the {THING}, the way they always seem to now.
The {THING} draws your attention once more.
long_look: You spend longer looking than usual. You don't remember deciding to stop.
You realise you've been standing in the aisle. For how long?
The train sways on, and you don't know how long you've been staring.

cutscene: Something in the coach changed, and you moved on past it.
If a coach is not exactly as you remember, turn back. If it is, keep moving on.
intro: The conductor clipped your ticket and nodded up the train. Coach 8, that way.
That was a while ago. You're still walking, and every carriage looks the same.
While a carriage is exactly as you remember it, keep walking forward.
If anything has changed, turn around. This one is folding you back.
Eight coaches, and you can finally sit down.
Lose your certainty, and the train sets you back where you started.
win: You slide open one more door, and the seat numbers read 8.
Coach 8. Your seat, empty and waiting, beside a black window.
You sink into it at last. The train rushes on through the night.

flashbacks: You had a seat once, further back, your coat over it. You got up to find coach 8.
Someone was meeting you at the end of the line. You cannot hold the name.
The sleeping passenger's coat is the same as yours. You do not look again.
The note scratched on the table is in your own hand. It says again, or it says eight.
You were sure the next stop was yours. The next stop is always the next stop.

exit.tempt: The train slows, and a door at the end of the coach opens onto a lit platform. Just step down and you are off.
exit.utterance: The passenger turns to you at last, and speaks in a voice you already know: "You have ridden past your stop."

platform: You step down onto the platform and the doors hiss shut behind you.
The train slides away into the tunnel without you.
The sign on the empty platform is a station you have never heard of, and the only service on the board is the train that just left.

reaction: Someone's folded shirts, a paperback, a ticket stub. Ordinary things, going somewhere.
It is heavier than it looks, and it does not want to open all the way.
herring: The tag's ink is still wet. It was probably always wet.
Your own initials are on the tag, in your hand. Common initials.
fold: Inside is the row of carriages, folded small and receding. When you look up you are at the first coach again.

reaction: Your reflection lags a half second behind you.
Black glass, a tunnel, and then a platform sliding by too fast to read.
herring: A handprint fogs and fades on the glass that is not yours. Or is.
Lights out there that could be a town, or could be the train, doubled.
fold: You watch the dark until it is all there is, and the coach resets around your reflection, back at the start.

reaction: Letters gouged into the laminate by a key or a nail. AGAIN, mostly. And a tally.
Under the newer marks, older ones, smoothed almost flat by hands.
herring: A ring of coffee still wet beside it, as if someone just left. People just left.
The scratches look raw, made minutes ago. Or polished by years. You cannot tell.
fold: You trace the tally with a finger and lose count, and the losing puts you back at coach one.

reaction: A murmur, no words you can keep. They do not wake.
They shift, and settle, and their breathing falls into step with yours.
herring: Their coat is the same cut as yours. Coats are coats.
For a second the face under the hood is turned toward you. Then it is not.
fold: They say your name in their sleep, clearly, once, and the sound of it carries you back to the first coach.
